commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes device pairing approval to reuse an existing same-key device auth token during metadata refreshes while adding regression tests for reuse and rotation boundaries.

PR surface: Source +58, Tests +135. Total +193 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a blank persisted token with otherwise valid role/scopes can pass the new reuse checks and be written back unchanged. I did not run tests because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical repair on the PR branch: reject malformed stored token values before reuse and add focused regression coverage.

target.title: fix(pairing): preserve stable device tokens on metadata refresh
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve existing untagged device tokens for same-key metadata refresh approvals when the token was already valid before the approval
- keep rotating for scope expansion, public-key repair, stale baseline tokens, revoked/issuer-tagged tokens, and malformed token role metadata
- add pairing token regression coverage for Windows node reconnect churn and unsafe reuse boundaries

## Validation
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts src/infra/device-pairing-churn.test.ts
- .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
- node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ... corepack pnpm check:changed (passed before final role-guard cleanup)
- OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled CI=1 node scripts/check-changed.mjs (passed guards/lint; tsgo skipped because sparse checkout lacks ui/config)

## Notes
Blacksmith delegated sync does not support --fresh-pr, and Crabbox sparse sync misdetected this sparse worktree as a broad deletion diff. The PR branch itself is a two-file diff.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
